pi_items.source: examples/nosh_env/tasks/check_in_patient/setup_task.sh line 13 (INSERT INTO demographics with lastname='Dent'). Note: Arthur Dent is a fictional character from 'The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy' by Douglas Adams (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arthur_Dent), not a real person.

Pending: setup_task.sh line 24 creates appointment with status='Pending'. No Show: process_patient_no_show/ task verifier and export scripts reference 'No Show' as valid status. Cancelled: reschedule_appointment/export_result.sh line 66-67 checks status != 'cancelled', confirming it is a valid NOSH status.

Both 'migraine' and 'aura' are real medical terms. Migraine with aura is a documented neurological condition defined by Mayo Clinic, Cleveland Clinic, and NCBI as a type of migraine accompanied by reversible focal neurological symptoms (https://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/migraine-with-aura/symptoms-causes/syc-20352072, https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/22131-migraine-aura).

privileged_info_summary: The task involves checking in a synthetically-created patient named Arthur Dent (a fictional character from science fiction literature) for an appointment at 10:00 AM in the NOSH ChartingSystem EHR at Hillside Family Medicine. The task requires changing the appointment status from 'Pending' to a non-excluded status (Arrived/Checked In) and updating the appointment reason to include the keywords 'migraine' and 'aura', which are real medical terms describing a documented neurological condition with visual and sensory disturbances. The system stores appointments with various status values including Pending, booked, arrived, seen, no_show, and cancelled. Patient data is synthetic (created by task setup scripts, not from actual patient records).
